title: Bridge Essay: Dreaming and the brain: from phenomenology to neurophysiology.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Dreaming, and, the, brain:, from.

**Dreaming and the brain: from phenomenology to neurophysiology.** — Trends Cogn Sci. PMID: 20079677. DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2009.12.001. Dreams are aremarkable experiment in psychology and neuroscience, conducted every night in every sleeping person. They show that the human brain, disconnected from the environment, can generate an entire world of conscious experiences by itself. Content analysis and developmental studies have promoted understanding of dream phenomenology. In parallel, brain lesion studies, functional imaging and neurophysiology have advanced current knowledge of the neural basis of dreaming. It is now possible
